threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'The Overground' instead of just the 'Gooner', and you avoid the vegan cafes because you haven't figured out the gluten-free hype yet.
Caught a guy singing Adele at the back of the 333 last night. Everyone was awkwardly trying not to clap, but you could see some were tempted.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I realized I truly lived in Neukölln when I saw an old man in a bathrobe chasing a runaway chicken down Warshawer Straße last summer.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Prenzlauer Berg will you find a spontaneous street wrestling match outside a café, complete with a DJ and a cheering crowd. Totally unexpected!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they navigate the bus schedule without checking their phone—it's like knowing the Muni routes by heart.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a woman bring her pet iguana on the 14 Mission last week. The driver didn’t bat an eye, just asked if it needed a seatbelt.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals are at the pessebre every holiday; meanwhile, we avoid it like the plague. It’s just too crowded now.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're fresh by their constant phone-checking while walking. Locals navigate with that innate sense of direction, no map needed.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's a 'charming stroll' to the high street, not a sprint to catch the last bus after dark.
Caught a late 61 last night, driver had the best playlist. It’s weird how the city feels more alive at 3 AM than any other time.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I saw a kid selling homemade lemonade in front of the Späti, while a group of older men debated the latest Bauprojekte. Classic Neukölln vibes.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a random street party that turns into an open-air rave, complete with local DJs spinning on balconies at 3 AM.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals know not to blink at the noise from the taco trucks at 2 AM, while newcomers clutch their phones like shields.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy in a full tuxedo getting off the Muni at 5 AM. He tipped his hat to the driver as he strolled off like it was prime time.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the real charm is in the little plazas where locals gossip over café con leche.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they fumble with the metro map, still trying to figure out the L2 stops between Sagrada Família and La Pau.
